words.def: a long period of time when there is little or no rain
words.example: Farmers are facing ruin after two years of severe drought.

words.def: not interesting or exciting
words.example: Life in a small town could be deadly dull.

words.def: to get rid of something you do not want, especially in a place that is not suitable
words.example: Too much toxic waste is being dumped at sea.

words.def: the length of time that something lasts or continues
words.example: The school was used as a hospital for the duration of the war.

words.def: the study of how a society organizes its money, trade and industry
words.example: He studied politics and economics at Yale.

words.def: a person who studies or writes about economics
words.example: the World Bank's chief economist

words.def: to prepare a piece of writing, a book, etc. to be published by correcting the mistakes, making improvements to it, etc.
words.example: edit (something), This draft text will need to be edited.

words.def: the form in which a book, newspaper, etc. is published
words.example: a paperback/hardback edition

words.def: connected with the task of preparing something such as a newspaper, a book or a television or radio programme, to be published or broadcast
words.example: the magazine’s editorial staff

words.def: having had the kind of education mentioned; having been to the school, college or university mentioned
words.example: privately educated children

words.def: in a way that produces the intended result or a successful result
words.example: The company must reduce costs to compete effectively.

words.def: doing something in a good, careful and complete way with no waste of time, money or energy
words.example: a highly efficient worker

words.def: in a good and careful way, with no waste of time, money or energy
words.example: a very efficiently organized event

words.def: the joint between the upper and lower parts of the arm where it bends in the middle
words.example: She jabbed him with her elbow.

words.def: used as a polite word for ‘old’
words.example: an elderly man/woman/lady

words.def: to choose somebody to do a particular job by voting for them
words.example: elect somebody/something, Voters will elect a new president on 30 March next year.

words.def: the branch of science and technology that studies electric currents in electronic equipment
words.example: the electronics industry

words.def: attractive and showing a good sense of style
words.example: She was tall and elegant.

words.def: in or connected with the first stages of a course of study
words.example: an elementary English course

words.def: to remove or get rid of something
words.example: eliminate something, Credit cards eliminate the need to carry a lot of cash.

words.def: in, at or to another place or other places
words.example: Dissatisfied customers will look elsewhere.

words.def: to put your arms around somebody as a sign of love or friendship
words.example: They embraced and promised to keep in touch.

words.def: to move out of or away from something and become possible to see
words.example: The crabs emerge at low tide to look for food.

words.def: the production or sending out of light, heat, gas, etc.
words.example: the emission of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere

words.def: connected with people’s feelings (= with the emotions)
words.example: emotional problems/stress

words.def: in a way that is connected with people's feelings (= with the emotions)
words.example: emotionally disturbed children

words.def: special importance that is given to something
words.example: emphasis on/upon something, Since the elections there has been a greater emphasis on education.

words.def: to give special importance to something
words.example: emphasize something, His speech emphasized the importance of attracting industry to the town.

words.def: a group of countries or states that are controlled by one leader or government
words.example: These invasions almost led to the collapse of the Roman Empire.

words.def: to give somebody a job to do for payment
words.example: employ somebody, How many people does the company employ?

words.def: a person who is paid to work for somebody
words.example: The firm has over 500 employees.

words.def: a person or company that pays people to work for them
words.example: They're very good employers (= they treat the people that work for them well).

words.def: to make it possible for somebody/something to do something
words.example: This new programme will enable older people to study at college.

words.def: a meeting, especially one that is sudden, unexpected or violent
words.example: a chance encounter

words.def: the strength, effort and enthusiasm required for physical or mental activity, work, etc.
words.example: It's a waste of time and energy.

words.def: to succeed in attracting and keeping somebody’s attention and interest
words.example: It is a movie that engages both the mind and the eye.

words.def: to increase or further improve the good quality, value or status of somebody/something
words.example: This is an opportunity to enhance the reputation of the company.

words.def: giving pleasure
words.example: an enjoyable weekend/experience

words.def: an official process to find out the cause of something or to find out information about something
words.example: a murder enquiry

words.def: to make sure that something happens or is definite
words.example: ensure something, We are working to ensure the safety of people in the city.

words.def: that you enjoy watching, listening to, doing or experiencing
words.example: an entertaining speech/evening

words.def: a strong feeling of excitement and interest in something and a desire to become involved in it
words.example: Her voice was full of enthusiasm.

words.def: feeling or showing a lot of excitement and interest about somebody/something
words.example: an enthusiastic supporter

words.def: including everything, everyone or every part
words.example: The entire village was destroyed.

words.def: in every way possible; completely
words.example: The experience was entirely new to me.

words.def: a person who makes money by starting or running businesses, especially when this involves taking financial risks
words.example: A creative entrepreneur, he was continually dreaming up new projects.

words.def: a flat paper container used for sending letters in, with a part that you stick down to close it
words.example: Writing paper and envelopes are provided in your room.

words.def: the natural world in which people, animals and plants live
words.example: The government should do more to protect the environment.

words.def: the same in size, quantity, value, etc. as something else
words.example: There is an equal number of boys and girls in the class.

words.def: to provide yourself/somebody/something with the things that are needed for a particular purpose or activity
words.example: equip something, to be fully/poorly equipped

words.def: equal in value, amount, meaning, importance, etc.
words.example: 250 grams or an equivalent amount in ounces

words.def: a period of time, usually in history, that is different from other periods because of particular characteristics or events
words.example: the Victorian/modern/post-war era

words.def: when a volcano erupts or burning rocks, smoke, etc. erupt or are erupted, the burning rocks, etc. are thrown out from the volcano
words.example: The volcano could erupt at any time.

words.def: when you think about the true, important or basic nature of somebody/something
words.example: There are three essentially different ways of tackling the problem.

words.def: to start or create an organization, a system, etc. that is meant to last for a long time
words.example: The committee was established in 1912.

words.def: an area of land with a lot of houses or factories of the same type on it
words.example: on an estate, She lives in a tower block on an estate in London.

words.def: a judgement that you make without having the exact details or figures about the size, amount, cost, etc. of something
words.example: to provide/obtain an estimate

words.def: moral principles that control or influence a person’s behaviour
words.example: professional/business/medical ethics

words.def: connected with beliefs and principles about what is right and wrong
words.example: ethical issues/standards/questions/dilemmas

words.def: connected with or belonging to a group of people that share a cultural tradition
words.example: ethnic background/origin

words.def: to form an opinion of the amount, value or quality of something after thinking about it carefully
words.example: evaluate something, The trial will evaluate the effectiveness of the different drugs.

words.def: the act of forming an opinion of the amount, value or quality of something after thinking about it carefully
words.example: an evaluation of the healthcare system

words.def: that can be divided exactly by two
words.example: 4, 6, 8 and 10 are all even numbers.

words.def: clear; easily seen
words.example: The orchestra played with evident enjoyment.

words.def: enjoying harming others; morally bad and cruel
words.example: Police described the killer as ‘a desperate and evil man’.

words.def: the slow steady development of plants, animals, etc. during the history of the earth, as they adapt to changes in their environment
words.example: the evolution of the human species

words.def: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complicated form; to develop something in this way
words.example: evolve (from something) (into something), The idea evolved from a drawing I discovered in the attic.

words.def: a formal written, spoken or practical test, especially at school or college, to see how much you know about a subject, or what you can do
words.example: successful candidates in GCSE examinations

words.def: to be greater than a particular number or amount
words.example: The price will not exceed £100.

words.def: a person or thing that is not included in a general statement
words.example: Most of the buildings in the town are modern, but the church is an exception.

words.def: greater than what seems reasonable or appropriate
words.example: They complained about the excessive noise coming from the upstairs flat.

words.def: to deliberately not include something in what you are doing or considering
words.example: The cost of borrowing has been excluded from the inflation figures.

words.def: a reason, either true or invented, that you give to explain or defend your behaviour
words.example: Late again! What's your excuse this time?
